cards.grammarNote: 가다 (gada) → 가요. The stem 가 ends in vowel 아, so + 아요 → merges to 가요. Movement away from speaker.
오다 (oda) → 와요. The vowel 오 + 아요 contracts to 와요. Movement toward speaker or listener.
가다 → 갔어요. Past tense of 가요. 갔어요 = went, have gone.
오다 → 왔어요. Past tense of 와요. 왔어요 = came, have come.
Future/intention: verb stem + ㄹ 거예요. 가 + ㄹ = 갈. 갈 거예요 = will go / am going to go.
오다 stem 오 + ㄹ = 올. 올 거예요 = will come. Note: 오 ends in vowel, so just add ㄹ.
Destination uses 에 (e). 학교에 가요 = I go to school. 에 marks the direction/destination.
와요 = come (toward speaker). 여기에 = to here. Often shortened to just 여기 와요 in speech.
어디 (eodi) = where. 어디 가요? is a very natural, common question.
언제 (eonje) = when. 언제 와요? asks when the listener will arrive at the speaker's location.
내일 (naeil) = tomorrow. 와요 implies moving toward where the listener/speaker is.
어디서 = from where (에서). 왔어요 = came (past of 오다). 에서 marks the origin.
미국에서 = from America. 왔어요 = came. This is the standard way to say where you're from.
같이 (gachi) = together. 가요 in this context acts as a suggestion/invitation: 'Let's go!'
Progressive: 가고 있어요 = am going. 지금 = now. Said by the person who is traveling toward someone.
오세요 is the polite imperative of 오다. 우리 집에 = to my/our house. 에 marks the destination.
Formal polite present of 가다. Used in formal announcements, news, official speech.
